title: Leptospermum minutifolium
intro: Leptospermum minutifolium, also known as the dwarf tea tree or small-leaved leptospermum, is a shrub native to Australia, characterized by its small, fragrant leaves and white or pink flowers. The essential oil extracted from its leaves has been traditionally used for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, providing potential health benefits such as wound healing and protection against infections. The bioactive constituents of Leptospermum minutifolium include a variety of compounds, including leptospermone, cineole, and limonene, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant's essential oil is commonly used in aromatherapy and is a key ingredient in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and topical creams.
